description_long: This object is a vintage CRT computer monitor with a bulky, rectangular form and a deep back housing. The front features a large, slightly curved glass display framed by a thick, off-white plastic bezel with gently rounded corners. The sides and rear are solid and boxy, with visible ventilation slats on both sides for heat dissipation. The base is integrated into the main body, slightly protruding and angled, providing stability. The surface is uniformly matte and off-white, with no visible branding or decorative elements. No buttons or ports are visible on the front or sides, but there are some small connectors at the lower rear edge.
description: A bulky, off-white vintage CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel and deep rear housing, featuring side vents and a matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the large, black display area framed by a thick, off-white bezel with rounded corners.
description_view_1: Right side view reveals the deep, boxy rear housing and vertical ventilation slats near the back.
description_view_2: Top view highlights the monitor's rectangular shape and the protruding rear section, with no visible controls or branding.
description_view_3: Left side view mirrors the right, showing the same deep housing and ventilation slats.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, off-white plastic with gently rounded corners and a smooth, matte finish. It is wide and frames the display area without any color contrast or decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: The stand is an integrated, slightly protruding base that is part of the main plastic housing, angled for stability and not visibly adjustable.
attribute_button_placement: No physical buttons or controls are visible on any side of the monitor.
attribute_port_visibility: A few small ports are visible at the lower rear edge of the monitor.
